reasoning:
To estimate the probability that the daily average temperature at Orly on February 7, 2026 (the presumed query date) will be higher than on November 9, 2025, I rely strictly on climatological base rates. Based on the 1991-2020 normal distributions for Paris (Orly), the mean daily average temperature on early November 9 is roughly 9.3°C (interpolating mid-October and mid-November), while on February 7 it is roughly 5.5°C (interpolating mid-January and mid-February). 

This requires the temperature on Feb 7 to overcome a mean deficit of ~3.8°C compared to Nov 9. Applying typical standard deviations for early winter daily temperatures in mid-latitudes (approx ~4.0°C to 4.5°C for each daily reading), the standard deviation of the difference between the two independent random days is roughly ~6.0°C. 

Calculating the Z-score for (T_Feb7 - T_Nov9 > 0): Z ≈ -3.8 / 6.0 = -0.63. Under a standard normal distribution, this corresponds to approximately a 24-26% chance that early February yields a warmer daily average than early November. The slight negative skew in European winter temperatures (due to periodic deep continental freezes) solidifies this estimate around 24%.
